perturbation.original_text: The Auction Platform will place Creative from the Target Demand with the Winning Bid Price on Google Ad Manager for such Creative to be placed on Customer's websites.
perturbation.changed_text: The Auction Platform will place Creative from the Target Demand on Customer's websites.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'with the Winning Bid Price', the contract now lacks a defined pricing mechanism, contradicting the purpose of the Auction Platform, which is designed to sell Inventory with a pre-agreed minimum bid. Section 3(j) refers to a 'Winning Bid Price', but Section 2 no longer mandates its use.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (j) "Winning Bid Price" means the price at which the Auction Platform awards Impressions to a bidding party which shall be no less than the minimum price that Customer has agreed to accept.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement that the Creative is placed with the 'Winning Bid Price' which directly contradicts Section 3(j) which defines 'Winning Bid Price' and implies its use. The omission in Section 2 makes Section 3(j) potentially irrelevant or unenforceable, creating a clear in-text contradiction as the pricing mechanism is no longer mandated.

perturbation.original_text: The services provided by Kubient pursuant to each specific engagement by Customer (the "Service" or "Services") shall be set forth and detailed in individual exhibits attached hereto (each an "Exhibit"), which shall be subject to the terms and conditions hereof and be incorporated as part of this Agreement. Kubient shall use reasonable efforts to provide to Customer the Services.
perturbation.changed_text: Kubient shall use reasonable efforts to provide to Customer the Services.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'The services provided by Kubient pursuant to each specific engagement by Customer (the "Service" or "Services") shall be set forth and detailed in individual exhibits attached hereto (each an "Exhibit"), which shall be subject to the terms and conditions hereof and be incorporated as part of this Agreement.', the specific details of the services are no longer defined within exhibits, but section 5 mentions 'each applicable Exhibit', creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties agree to share any revenue generated as a result of this Agreement, or Customer's use of the Services or the Auction Platform, as set forth and detailed in each applicable Exhibit. Unless specified otherwise in an Exhibit, Kubient shall be responsible for contracting with all advertisers, agencies, media buyers, and/or sponsors (collectively "Advertisers") on Customer's behalf, including Advertisers with whom Customer has existing relationships. Pursuant to the doctrine of sequential liability, payment from Kubient to Customer shall be made within seven (7) days from Kubient's receipt of payment, regardless of when Customer submits an invoice. Customer acknowledges and agrees that Kubient shall proceed with recovery of the amounts due on a best effort basis but will not initiate any legal action against a delinquent party without the express written consent and support of Customer. Except of taxes associated with Kubient's income, Customer shall be responsible for paying all applicable sales, use or other taxes, duties, or tariffs applicable to its use of the Services.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the services provided by Kubient will be detailed in individual exhibits. By removing this clause in the changed text, the contract no longer defines where the details of the services are located. The contradicted text then references 'each applicable Exhibit' to define revenue sharing. Because the changed text removes the requirement for exhibits, it contradicts the contradicted text's assumption that exhibits will exist and contain revenue details, creating an in-text contradiction, as the exhibits are no longer defined, making the reference to them inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, if Recipient is required by applicable Law or a valid legal order to disclose any Confidential Information, Recipient shall, before such disclosure, notify Discloser of such requirements, if legally permissible and reasonably practicable, so that Discloser may seek a protective order or other remedy, and Recipient shall reasonably assist Discloser therewith (at Discloser's cost).
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, if Recipient is required by a valid legal order to disclose any Confidential Information, Recipient shall, before such disclosure, notify Discloser of such requirements so that Discloser may seek a protective order or other remedy.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'applicable Law or', and 'if legally permissible and reasonably practicable, and Recipient shall reasonably assist Discloser therewith (at Discloser's cost)', the obligation to notify if legally permissible and assist at the discloser's cost is removed, which creates an in-text contradiction because it removes the clause that was considering to comply or assist due to applicable Law.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient will protect the Confidential Information, for one (1) year from the date of disclosure, by using at least the same degree of care as it uses to protect its own Confidential Information, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, to prevent unauthorized use, disclosure or publication. Notwithstanding the foregoing, if Recipient is required by a valid legal order to disclose any Confidential Information, Recipient shall, before such disclosure, notify Discloser of such requirements so that Discloser may seek a protective order or other remedy. If Recipient remains legally compelled to make such disclosure, it shall only disclose that portion of the Confidential Information Recipient is required to disclose. Without limiting the foregoing, Recipient: (x) will not use, disclose, make available or reproduce the Confidential Information (or permit others to do so) except as expressly authorized in this Agreement; (y) will not disclose any such Confidential Information to anyone except employees and directors of Recipient to whom disclosure is necessary for the performance of the Agreement; and (z) will appropriately notify such employees and directors that the disclosure is made in confidence and will be kept in confidence in accordance with this Agreement. If Recipient becomes aware of any loss or unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information, Recipient will promptly notify Discloser of such and use reasonable efforts to retrieve such Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly considered situations where disclosure might be required by law but notification or assistance was not permissible or practical. By removing "applicable Law or" and "if legally permissible and reasonably practicable, and Recipient shall reasonably assist Discloser therewith (at Discloser's cost)", the modified text creates a contradiction because it imposes an unconditional notification requirement, where as the Contradicted Text also uses this conditionality and considers that the notification is a general requirement, which goes against the original agreement.
